title: 2 N.H. residents were on cruise ship that quarantined over hantavirus, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two New Hampshire residents were on the MV Hondius cruise ship during a hantavirus outbreak, state officials reported.
Three passengers died from the hantavirus outbreak on the ship, which docked in Tenerife, Canary Islands on May 10.
The New Hampshire residents are back in the U.S., but not in New Hampshire, with ongoing health monitoring planned.

title: Red Sox might have to make a decision on Trevor Story amid veteran’s struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story is batting .200 with two home runs and 18 RBI this season, posing challenges for the Red Sox.
Story's defensive performance includes five errors, contributing to Boston's weak start at 17-23 in 2026.
Potential Red Sox strategies include switching Story's position or even trading or designating him for assignment.

title: Lynn mother, educator avoids deportation for now
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mariola, a Guatemalan mother and educator in Lynn, avoided deportation after an ICE check-in, pending a 'Reasonable Fear Interview.'
Mariola came to the US 16 years ago due to threats in Guatemala. She faces systemic violence as an indigenous Mayan, her attorney says.
The Lynn community, including Mayor Jared Nicholson, supports Mariola, who is raising a US-born son and pursuing a degree at Salem State.

title: Night Shift’s outdoor beer gardens are back for the 2026 season. Here’s what to know.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Night Shift Brewing's beer gardens reopen this week at Esplanade, Herter Park, and Dewey Square in Boston.
New offerings include Rickey Sour ale and Mai Day lager; regular and seasonal beverages also available.
Beer gardens feature dog-friendly seating, rotating food trucks, and operate on a walk-in basis without reservations.

title: Sail Boston 250 is coming up. Here’s where to learn to sail.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250 will take place from July 11 to 16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries.
Several Boston-area sailing schools offer courses for beginners to advanced sailors in preparation for Sail Boston 250.
Notable sailing schools include Boston Sailing Center and Courageous Sailing, offering accredited and certified courses.

title: Suspected Memorial Drive gunman’s criminal record comes under scrutiny after Cambridge shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown, 46, is accused of firing over 50 rounds on Memorial Drive, injuring two people on May 11, 2026.
Brown was on probation for a 2020 incident where he fired at South End police; critics say his sentences were too lenient.
Political figures criticize the criminal justice system's handling of violent offenders, citing inadequate sentencing and parole decisions.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest for a limited time
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest offers one-way flights starting at $59 until May 14 for domestic and international travel.
The sale applies to flights on select days with travel valid from June 2 to November 18.
Deals from Boston include $59 to Baltimore, with tickets requiring a 21-day advance purchase.

title: Parts of Storrow Drive, Soldiers Field Road to face nightly closures
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nightly closures on Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will occur from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m. through August.
The closures span from North Harvard Street in Allston to Mugar Way in Boston for tunnel maintenance project work.
Detours involve Anderson Bridge to Memorial Drive, reentering Boston via Longfellow Bridge; speed reduction urged.

title: Self-serve ticket machine yields $10 million winner at Boston supermarket
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A $10 million lottery ticket was purchased at a Dorchester Stop & Shop self-service terminal.
Rock Solid Trust claimed the prize, opting for a $6.5 million one-time cash payout before taxes.
Stop & Shop at 545 Freeport St. received a $50,000 bonus for selling the winning ticket.

title: Equestrian retailer Dover Saddlery warns of more than 100 potential layoffs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Dover Saddlery may lay off 112 employees at its Littleton facility from July 7 to July 10 due to financial strains.
Founded by the Powers brothers in 1975, Dover Saddlery has grown into a national equestrian retailer with 37 stores.
Promus Equity Partners acquired Dover Saddlery in 2022; the retailer secured $15 million financing in 2024 for growth plans.
